prerequisites: A Humanitix account with access to the Console (https://console.humanitix.com).
Permission to view the Advanced account settings, where the Public API key is generated and managed.
A generated Public API key, copied for use in the api_key field. Generating a new key invalidates any existing one.

authMethods.name: API key (x-api-key header)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single Humanitix Public API key supplied in the required api_key field. Humanitix expects this key in an x-api-key request header on every call to the API (base URL https://api.humanitix.com/v1). The key grants access to account data, so keep it secret.

setupSteps.title: Generate your Humanitix Public API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Humanitix Console at https://console.humanitix.com. From the top menu, go to Account > Advanced > Public API key (direct link: https://console.humanitix.com/console/account/advanced/api-key). Generate the key and copy it. Note that generating a new key breaks any previously issued key.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied key into the connector's required api_key field. This value is sent as the x-api-key header on every request, which is how Humanitix authenticates the connection. There are no other configuration fields to set.

setupSteps.title: Verify the connection
setupSteps.body: Save the configuration and run a connection check. The connector calls the Humanitix Public API (https://api.humanitix.com/v1) using the x-api-key header; a successful response confirms the key is valid. Note that Humanitix enforces a rate limit of 200 requests per minute.

sources.title: Public API documentation - Humanitix Help Centre
Humanitix Python API Docs - dltHub (base URL and x-api-key header)
